summary: Defined the unified intake contract that normalizes Fireflies, Fathom, Pocket, and recovered historical content into one canonical landing shape in Personal/Meetings/Inbox/. Resolved DP-1 (single schema with content_type discriminator for meeting vs reflection) and DP-2 (9 minimum ready-to-process checks including manifest, transcript, date, title, source, and content_type). Contract covers: content type routing rules with explicit Pocket default-to-reflection logic, canonical folder structure, manifest schema at intake, provenance fields for recovered items, 5 source adapters (fireflies_v1, fathom_v1, pocket_v1, recovery_v1, manual_v1), ready-to-process validation with reference implementation, landing state definitions, transcript normalization rules, and schema extension points.

decisions.DP-1: Single schema with content_type discriminator (meeting | reflection). One pipeline, one landing shape, one validation path. Reflection has relaxed requirements (no participants, no calendar match, no meeting.type). Avoids maintaining parallel schemas for content that shares 80%+ of structure.
decisions.DP-2: 9 minimum ready-to-process checks: folder exists, manifest exists, schema version is manifest-v3, content_type set, status is ingested or later, transcript.md exists with >=300 chars, meeting.date set, meeting.title set, source.type and source.adapter set. Participants, calendar match, quality gate, and blocks are NOT required at intake — they are populated by later pipeline stages.

learnings: The existing manifest-v3 schema has no content_type field — it will need to be extended to add the meeting/reflection discriminator. The schema currently uses meeting.type for internal/external, which is orthogonal to content_type.

source.adapter field is new — current manifests have source.type but not source.adapter. Adapters that wrote historical manifests should be treated as implicit 'legacy_v2' or 'recovery_v1' adapter versions.

Pocket content type routing needs a concrete speaker-detection implementation. The contract defines the rules but the actual multi-speaker detection threshold (2+ distinct labels, duration > 5min with turns) needs to be implemented in the pocket_v1 adapter.

Recovered items that already have generated blocks should enter the pipeline at 'processed' status, not 'ingested' — this avoids re-running expensive block generation on content that was already fully processed in the historical system.
